The second signal system runs on 15-minute bars using a single 233-period exponential moving average. When price crosses the EMA intrabar — meaning the current bar's high exceeds the 233 EMA after the prior bar's close was below it — enter immediately, a couple cents above the EMA level to avoid the frequent rejections that occur right at the line. No confirmation bar or continuation filter required. Simply enter at the cross, then let a 20% take profit and 20% stop loss manage the trade from there. An opposite signal (intrabar cross to the downside after prior close was above EMA) closes any existing position and reverses. Open positions that haven't hit either the 20% TP or SL by 15:55 ET are closed out at market.
